Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JACQUELINE OH, Esq. of the Veen Firm, P.C. (Tel: 415-673-4800) for plaintiff William Bostwick;
Atty. DALE RATNER, Esq. of Bishop / Barry (Tel: 510-596-0888) for defendants Big Dog City Corporation dba Yellow Cab of San Francisco, and Santiago Farias.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BIG DOG CITY CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY HAS NOW BEEN REDUCED TO SEEKING AN ORDER TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 4 THAT MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO HAVE A CONTENTION IN REGARD TO THE ALTERNATE STOPPING AND PICKUP PLACE. I ALSO WOULD HAVE SUSTAINED AN OBJECTION ON THE GROUND THAT THE INTERROGATORY IS VAGUE AND AMBIGUOUS.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order, serve it on defense counsel for approval as to form, and then submit it to the pro tem judge by email sent to rennepar85@gmail.com.
